title: Richard Coer de Lyon
summary: The checked-in CME00049 XML represents the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse text keyed from Karl Brunner's 1913 critical edition of Richard Coer de Lyon. Brunner constructed his continuous a-version reading chiefly from Gonville and Caius College MS 175/96 (C), supplied its gaps from Wynkyn de Worde's 1509 print (W1), and selectively printed divergent b-version passages from College of Arms MS HDN 58 (A) while collating the other witnesses and the 1528 print. The XML preserves Brunner's edited text, extensive apparatus, and an alternative W/A ending, but not the entire 604-page edition. A complete scan of Brunner, complete public digital inventories for Bodleian MS Douce 228 and the dispersed Auchinleck witness, a complete EEBO-TCP transcription of the 1509 print, and complete modern supporting editions are cached. The manuscript bundles exhaust the public provider inventories found but remain partial at physical-object level because their providers omit surfaces.
review_notes: The intellectual subject is the anonymous Middle English verse romance or crusading poem Richard Coer de Lyon, not Richard I as a historical person, any single witness codex, Brunner's critical edition, the U-M encoding, or a facsimile.

The textual tradition is fluid. Brunner groups C, B, W1, and W2 as version a and A, H, L, E, and D as version b; later scholarship treats those groupings as useful but does not establish that one surviving witness is copied directly from another.

The XML contains 8,064 L elements: 7,268 direct reading-text lines and 796 apparatus-nested witness readings. Its 7,212-line main reading is followed by a 56-line alternative ending that replaces the passage beginning at Brunner line 7203 rather than extending the work to 7,268 sequential numbered lines.

Whole-manuscript caching follows the complete provider object where practical. The 80-image Douce bundle covers every published medieval folio face, and the 701-image Auchinleck bundle covers the complete legacy project inventory; both are marked partial at physical-object level and explicitly identify surfaces their providers did not publish.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00049
notes: The pinned file is 2,028,689 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00049
notes: The encoding includes Brunner's title page, part title, Einrichtung der Ausgabe on printed pp. 79–80, the main critical text on pp. 81–450, and the alternative W/A ending on pp. 450–452.
It omits Brunner's introduction on pp. 1–78, post-text annotations on pp. 453–459, word list on pp. 460–461, names index on pp. 462–474, and German translation on pp. 475–604.

entities.label: Karl Brunner's Der mittelenglische Versroman über Richard Löwenherz (1913)

bibliographic.citation: Karl Brunner, ed., Der mittelenglische Versroman über Richard Löwenherz: kritische Ausgabe nach allen Handschriften mit Einleitung, Anmerkungen und deutscher Übersetzung, Wiener Beiträge zur englischen Philologie 42 (Wien and Leipzig: Wilhelm Braumüller, 1913).
bibliographic.title: Der mittelenglische Versroman über Richard Löwenherz

notes: The critical text occupies pp. 81–452; Brunner's main reading is the a-version, based principally on C and supplemented from W1 where C is defective.
Divergent b-version passages appear below the main text in italics, based on A where possible; purely orthographic variants were normally omitted.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Brunner 1913
entities.reproduction_format: 628-page PDF with Internet Archive metadata and scan-data sidecars
notes: The scan contains the complete provider volume, not merely the CME-encoded pages.

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 175/96

holding.notes: Richard Coer de Lyon occupies manuscript pp. 1–98; the text has losses after pp. 4, 8, 24, and 94.

notes: Brunner's principal continuous a-version base; the work is incomplete through lost leaves.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 31042 (London Thornton Manuscript)

holding.notes: Richard Coer de Lyon occupies ff. 125r–163v.

physical_description.support: paper, with four parchment leaves from a breviary
notes: Copied by Robert Thornton; leaves are missing or mutilated within the work.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 2862

holding.notes: Richard Coer de Lyon occupies ff. 1r–44v and is defective at both ends.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4690

holding.notes: The current BL foliation places the work at ff. 109r–118r; older scholarship often cites ff. 106–115.

physical_description.extent: 118 folios plus three front and two rear unfoliated flyleaves; work at current ff. 109r–118r
notes: The surviving text is incomplete.

entities.label: College of Arms MS HDN 58

holding.notes: The poem occupies approximately ff. 252r–275; secondary descriptions sometimes give the broader embedded context as ff. 250–275.

notes: The 3,686-line surviving poem lacks its beginning and has a cut ending; Brunner preferred A for b-version divergences where available.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 228

holding.notes: Richard Coer de Lyon occupies the entire medieval folio block, ff. 1r–40v; f. 35 is misbound and should follow f. 39.

physical_description.extent: five later front leaves + 40 medieval folios + one later rear leaf
notes: The poem is imperfect at beginning and end.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian published folio inventory for MS Douce 228
entities.reproduction_format: 80-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG image sequence
notes: The public inventory covers fol. 1r through fol. 40v. It omits the five later front leaves, later rear leaf, binding exteriors, boards, spine, pastedowns, edges, and other physical surfaces, so physical-object coverage is partial even though the cached bundle exhausts all 80 published canvases.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript main fragment)

holding.notes: The NLS portions of Richard Coer de Lyon are ff. 326r–327v; detached continuation leaves survive in Edinburgh and St Andrews.

entities.label: Edinburgh University Library MS 218 (detached Auchinleck leaves)

holding.notes: Richard Coer de Lyon occupies detached leaves ff. 3r–4v.

physical_description.extent: four detached leaves total; Richard at ff. 3r–4v

entities.label: University of St Andrews Library PR2065.R4 (detached Auchinleck leaves)

holding.notes: Richard Coer de Lyon occupies ff. 1r–2v.

notes: These leaves were discovered after Brunner's edition and therefore did not contribute to his 1913 collation.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland Auchinleck Manuscript digital project
entities.reproduction_format: 701 publicly exposed JPEG image resources assembled from the project inventory
notes: The project aggregates the NLS main codex and detached Edinburgh and St Andrews fragments. Its 701-image inventory is complete as published but omits complete binding exteriors, boards, spine, pastedowns, every endleaf, blank, stub, edge, and physical surface.

entities.label: Gloucestershire Archives D2800/V/1 no. 8 (Badminton fragment)

holding.notes: One inner bifolium survives; the fragment is too short to assign securely to Brunner's a or b version.

physical_description.extent: one inner bifolium, approximately 160 legible lines

notes: The fragment was unknown to Brunner and is a later addition to the witness census.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde's 1509 printing of Richard Coer de Lyon

bibliographic.citation: Richard Coer de Lyon (London: Wynkyn de Worde, 1509), STC 21007; ESTC S120269; USTC 501039.

notes: This schema type represents an historical printed textual witness, not a modern critical edition. Brunner used the Bodleian copy, shelfmark Crynes 734, to supply gaps in C.

entities.label: EEBO-TCP A10728 transcription of the 1509 printing
notes: This is a complete encoded transcription of the volume, not a page-image facsimile. Its CC0 waiver expressly does not cover EEBO page images.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde's 1528 printing of Richard Coer de Lyon

bibliographic.citation: Richard Coer de Lyon (London: Wynkyn de Worde, 1528), STC 21008; ESTC S101588; USTC 502059.

notes: This schema type represents an historical printed textual witness. Brunner collated W2 for non-orthographic differences but used W1 for the principal supplements to C.

entities.label: Mary Figueredo's The Medieval Text of Richard Coer de Lion (2009)

bibliographic.citation: Mary Figueredo, The Medieval Text of Richard Coer de Lion: An Edition Based on British Library MS Additional 31042, 2 vols. (PhD thesis, University of York, 2009).
bibliographic.title: The Medieval Text of Richard Coer de Lion

notes: Volume 1 is a study of the tradition and includes 54 selected plates; it is not a complete manuscript facsimile. Volume 2 edits the London Thornton text.

rights.component: U-M CME00049 item and encoded textual materials
rights.basis: The official U-M item page labels the materials public domain and links the RightsStatements.org No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: This does not determine rights in independently supplied modern facsimile images.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1913, and the U-M item made from it is explicitly marked No Copyright–United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and metadata
rights.basis: The underlying 1913 edition is public domain in the United States, but the inspected Internet Archive metadata did not supply a separate open license for all scan-layer files.
notes: Public download is recorded separately from permission to redistribute provider-created scan files.

rights.basis: The IIIF manifest and item attribution identify Photo © Bodleian Libraries and license the images under CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: Attribution is required. Commercial redistribution, including profit-oriented print-on-demand use, is outside this license and requires separate permission.

rights.component: NLS Auchinleck project images and site materials
rights.basis: The project's official terms restrict materials to personal use and prohibit unauthorized commercial use or publication.
notes: The ignored local cache is for research preservation and should not be redistributed from the Git repository without permission.

rights.basis: The repository README applies the CC0 1.0 waiver to the TCP-encoded text and explicitly excludes page images.
notes: The waiver cannot be transferred to EEBO/ProQuest page images.

rights.component: 2009 thesis text, edition, and selected plates
rights.basis: The modern thesis is publicly downloadable, but no open redistribution license was verified on the item or files.
notes: Public access does not by itself authorize republication of the thesis or its manuscript plates.

rights.component: 2015 editorial text, introduction, notes, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: This is a modern scholarly edition; public reading and download were verified but no license authorizing redistribution was found.

editorial_practices.source_feature: fluid a- and b-version textual tradition
editorial_practices.representation: continuous a-version reading with selected b-version divergences below
editorial_practices.description: Brunner prints C as the continuous base, supplements C from W1, and places materially divergent b-version passages below the line, using A where possible.

editorial_practices.source_feature: then-known manuscripts and two early prints
editorial_practices.description: Brunner collated every witness then known to him, but normally omitted purely orthographic variants and did not know the later St Andrews fragment or Badminton fragment.

editorial_practices.representation: expanded in the printed reading text
editorial_practices.description: Brunner states that abbreviations are expanded; the CME text consequently represents editorial expansions rather than manuscript abbreviation shapes.
The printed punctuation belongs to Brunner's editorial presentation and should not be treated as a diplomatic image of any witness.

editorial_practices.representation: encoded title and editorial-method pages, critical text, apparatus, and alternate ending
editorial_practices.description: U-M encoded the source-edition portion surrounding the Middle English text and apparatus, not Brunner's complete introduction, notes, glossary, index, or German translation.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME submodule, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00049.xml
evidence.summary: Establishes repository identity, U-M identifiers, source-edition title, encoded page ranges, critical reading text, apparatus, and alternative ending.

evidence.citation: Computational inspection of CME00049 XML structure
evidence.locator: XPath counts for DIV1, DIV2, PB, NOTE, and direct versus NOTE-nested L
evidence.summary: Finds 5 DIV1, 1 DIV2, 377 PB, 8,167 NOTE, and 8,064 L elements; 7,268 L are direct reading lines and 796 are apparatus-nested readings. The main section has 7,212 direct lines and the alternative ending has 56.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00049
evidence.summary: Provides the public CME item, persistent identifier, source attribution to Brunner, and No Copyright–United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Karl Brunner, Der mittelenglische Versroman über Richard Löwenherz (1913)
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for bibliographic identity, sigla, critical text, page ranges, and source witnesses.

evidence.citation: Karl Brunner, Einrichtung der Ausgabe
evidence.summary: Explains the C-based a-version reading, W1 supplements, A-based b-version divergences, witness sigla, collation policy, abbreviation expansion, and omission of purely orthographic variants.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item dermittelenglisc00brun
evidence.summary: Provides the exact complete PDF, metadata, and scan-data downloads for Brunner's volume.

evidence.citation: Local inspection of cached Brunner 1913 files
evidence.summary: Verifies file hashes, byte sizes, page count, and the PDF mappings for title, method, main text, and alternative ending.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Richard Coeur de Lion
evidence.summary: Provides the conventional title, circa-1300 composition date, tentative London localization, rhyming-couplet form, NIMEV number, witness census and folio ranges, and Brunner source citation.

evidence.citation: Peter Larkin, Introduction to Richard Coer de Lyon, Middle English Texts Series
evidence.summary: Current scholarly introduction describing the crusading romance, a/b versions, manuscripts and prints, fluid textual tradition, source questions, and themes.

evidence.citation: Peter Larkin, Richard Coer de Lyon bibliography, Middle English Texts Series
evidence.summary: Bibliography for modern source, manuscript, edition, and critical research.

evidence.citation: Richard Coer de Lyon Multitext project
evidence.summary: Provides a current witness list including the Badminton fragment, cautions that no surviving manuscript is simply the source of another, and contextualizes the poem's anti-Muslim violence and modern racist appropriation.
notes: Per-witness pages were empty placeholders when checked and are not treated as facsimiles or digitization evidence.

evidence.citation: Mary Figueredo, The Medieval Text of Richard Coer de Lion, vol. 1 (PhD thesis, University of York, 2009)
evidence.summary: Detailed modern study and descriptions of all principal manuscript witnesses and the Badminton fragment; also reviews composition and source hypotheses.

evidence.citation: Mary Figueredo, The Medieval Text of Richard Coer de Lion, vol. 2 (PhD thesis, University of York, 2009)
evidence.summary: Modern edition based on British Library Add MS 31042.

open_questions.question: Can complete whole-object facsimiles be obtained for C, B, E, H, A, the Badminton fragment, and the 1509 and 1528 print copies?
next_steps: Request whole-object quotations from each holding institution, explicitly including all text leaves, blanks, endleaves, pastedowns, binding exteriors, spine, edges, mounts, enclosures, and color targets where feasible.
For BL holdings, submit separate requests for Add MS 31042, Egerton MS 2862, and Harley MS 4690 through the current digitisation service.
Record any returned terms, exact scope, identifiers, omitted surfaces, and publication restrictions without treating a work-only range as a whole manuscript.

open_questions.question: Will the Bodleian supply images of the physical surfaces omitted from the 80-canvas Douce 228 inventory?
next_steps: Request the five later front leaves, one later rear leaf, boards, spine, binding exteriors, pastedowns, edges, and any other uncaptured surfaces.
If supplied, cache the complete received object separately and retain the public 80-canvas bundle's narrower coverage statement.

open_questions.question: Can the NLS, Edinburgh, and St Andrews supply complete physical-object photography beyond the legacy Auchinleck project's 701-image inventory?
next_steps: Ask each current holder separately for all physical surfaces of its object, not only Richard leaves.
Preserve the reconstructed textual order separately from the provider inventory order and physical custody order.
Seek explicit permission before publishing any supplied images.

open_questions.question: How should the a- and b-version witnesses, early prints, and later-found fragments be related without imposing a false linear stemma?
next_steps: Consult current stemmatic and multitext scholarship and compare complete witnesses directly.
Keep Brunner's a/b classification scoped to his editorial method and avoid asserting direct copying among surviving witnesses.
Treat the St Andrews and Badminton fragments as modern supporting witnesses rather than unacknowledged inputs to Brunner.
